message:
feat(scheduler/three_lane): integrate drain progress certificate for stall detection

Wire a martingale-based ProgressCertificate into the three-lane scheduler's
governor loop to provide statistical convergence verdicts during obligation
drain phases.

Certificate integration:
- Add `drain_certificate: Option<ProgressCertificate>` field to
  ThreeLaneWorker, initialized with ProgressCertificate::with_defaults()
  when the Lyapunov governor is enabled.
- Feed the current Lyapunov potential to the certificate on every governor
  tick via cert.observe(potential), producing a DrainVerdict with
  Azuma-Hoeffding + Freedman concentration bounds and drain-phase
  classification (Warmup / RapidDrain / SlowTail / Stalled / Quiescent).

Scheduler actuation:
- When the certificate detects a stall (potential not decreasing for
  stall_threshold consecutive snapshots), escalate the scheduling
  suggestion to DrainObligations to force progress. This closes the
  feedback loop from statistical observation to scheduling action.
- When the certificate reaches Quiescent phase (drain converged), relax
  back to NoPreference and reset the certificate for the next drain cycle.
- Critical safety invariant: never override a trapped-wait-cycle forced
  drain — the certificate's quiescence verdict does NOT mean a structural
  deadlock is resolved, since blocked tasks may have zero potential while
  remaining permanently stuck.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
